title: Ecbolium geniculatum
intro: Ecbolium geniculatum, also known as the Fingered Ecbolium, is a shrub-like perennial plant native to tropical Africa. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its various health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of fever, rheumatism, and skin infections. The bioactive constituents of Ecbolium geniculatum include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made from Ecbolium geniculatum include infusions, decoctions, and ointments, which are used to treat a range of health conditions.
taxonomy: Ecbolium geniculatum is a species of plant in the family Acanthaceae. It belongs to the genus Ecbolium, which consists of flowering plants native to tropical Africa. The plant is classified as a member of the order Lamiales and the clade Asterids. Ecbolium geniculatum is a shrub-like plant with distinctive characteristics and is often found in woodland and savannah habitats. Further classification of Ecbolium geniculatum is also possible at the subfamily, tribe, and family levels.

Studies have shown that the plant contains compounds with antimicrobial and antifungal activities, which can be beneficial in treating infections.
